title: 'Severe heatwave in state, everyone must exercise caution, drink plenty of water even if you do not feel thirsty' - Kerala Kaumudi
full_text:
THIRUVANANTHAPURAM: Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan has urged people to exercise caution as temperatures continue to rise sharply across the state. In a Facebook post, he said that the warning issued by the weather department about temperatures reaching up to 40°C in districts including Palakkad should be taken seriously.
Chief Minister's Facebook post
In view of the sharp rise in temperatures across the state, everyone must exercise extreme caution. The warning issued by the weather department that temperatures may rise up to 40°C in districts, including Palakkad, should be taken seriously. People should avoid direct exposure to sunlight as much as possible between 11 a.m. and 3 p.m. It is important to drink plenty of water even if you do not feel thirsty. Special care should be taken for children, the elderly, and pregnant women.
During extreme heat, reptiles may enter populated areas and even homes in search of cooler conditions. We have recently seen the tragic incident in Kodaly, Thrissur, where an eight-year-old boy died after being bitten by a snake while sleeping. I express my deep sorrow over the incident and share the grief of the family and the community.
This is an example that shows how extreme heat can lead to unexpected dangers. Children often go to ponds and rivers to cool off during hot weather, and caution must be exercised in such places as well. By strictly following the safety guidelines issued by the State Disaster Management Authority, we can safely get through this severe summer season.
